_comment: End-to-end feature/physics-parity test, run against BOTH the original Flash projector (tools/pr2driver.py) and the OpenFL HTML5 port (tools/openfl_driver.py) from this one file. Identical inputs for both clients (no per-client branching). Flow: preloader/intro skip -> open Login -> clear any saved session via the '-' button so both clients land on the same name/pass form -> type testjun6/testjun6 -> Log In -> favorites (heart) tab -> 'Don't Move JV' -> Play -> idle while the level's blocks push the character through the course on their own -> Race Complete! popup. The '-' (delete saved user) + confirm-OK clicks are no-ops on the fresh name/pass form (they land on empty dialog area), so the sequence works whether or not a session is saved. Shot 'out' paths use {target} (replaced with 'flash' or 'openfl' by each driver) so both runs land in separate dirs as comparable 550x400 PNGs. The gameplay shotSeries writes one screenshot per second under dmjv-{target}/gameplay/ so the Flash baseline and OpenFL run can be compared step-by-step through the level. Times are seconds from app start (Flash window-ready after launch / OpenFL data-pr2-app-ready). 'launch'/'quit' are no-ops on the OpenFL driver. The port must be served through tools/dev_proxy.py (apiHost=/api) so the server list and login reach pr2hub.com. Assert the final shot with tools/check_race_complete.py.

steps._note: skip Jiggmin intro anim if present (harmless empty click otherwise)
'-' delete saved user (drops a remembered session so both clients show the name/pass form); no-op on the fresh form
confirm OK on the delete-account dialog; harmless stray click on empty dialog area when no confirm appeared
favorites: 'Don't Move JV' level slot (below the byline -> Cancel/Play popup)
one screenshot per second through the level, from level entry to the expected Race Complete window
~96s after Play: Race Complete! popup should be up
close the projector (no-op on OpenFL; final shot already saved)
